evidence_quotes.quote: AI chatbot that lead to the FAQ, while the FAQ lead to the AI chatbot in a never-ending cycle
A lot of sites are asking for way too many sensitive informations
some platforms just delete in bulk products that you bought (like Crunchyroll)
other apply changes to the contract after your payament and you have to accept it, or lose what you bought.
not really owning the product that you buy online

paragraphs.text: Thanks for this opportunity; I would like to add and point out that sending a feedback and getting in touch with customer support is really hard nowadays. A lot of companies simply hide this information, sometimes it doesn't exists at all. Talking to a human from the support is impossible in many cases. An example of this are telecommunications companies, which hide behind a AI chatbot that lead to the FAQ, while the FAQ lead to the AI chatbot in a never-ending cycle where you have to fake to be a new potential customer in order to get a number to talk to a real human and ask for a solution to your problem. The fact that the support is frequently online-only and only from a chatbot means that if my problem is not having a internet connection, I can't get help from anyone unless I trick the wrong department into helping me. All of that leaving aside that if I can't call, nor connect to internet, it's totally impossible to send a complaint: certain companies never provide a physical address to get support, nor a chat with a real human, nor a dedicated number to call for help. Without internet I can't even check where is the closest physical shop to ask them, and even in such place they can't help you because "they are just sellers". It happened to me and to several friends to have to borrow someone else phone to call a not-free service not related to the support to get help because customer support just doesn't exists anymore. All of this can be applied to other kind of companies, including social network and other service providers, since they make really hard contacting a human from the support team to fix a problem that it's impossible to solve just reading FAQs. How they manage security is concerning as well, for example Yahoo! Mail had been hacked in the past, even hospitals got hacked in my country, some platforms just delete in bulk products that you bought (like Crunchyroll), other apply changes to the contract after your payament and you have to accept it, or lose what you bought. A lot of sites are asking for way too many sensitive informations, despite they are private companies and they shouldn't asking me to get identified, since they are not the police, they are not public officials, they are complete strangers with no reason to force me to do that. I don't trust them, and I wish to avoid giving my data to complete strangers. Another importal thing is the censorship applied by credit card companies; recently they forced some platforms (like Steam, Itch) into deleting/removing part of their product for moral reasons, despite they were legal and this should not be possible. If something it's illegal, it should be reported to the police, and credit card companies are not a court judge. They also made Patreon censor its content and its users, despite their art was legal. It affected also companies related to anime and manga, like what happened recently in Japan. It should not be possible for a credit card company to choose what I can or I can not buy; what they are doing in fact can force entire companies into bankrupt, because if they don't process payment for them, they can't get any money, nor pay anyone and in the end they'll get in debt and they'll have to close, leaving a lot of people without a job. Another things that I wish to remind to, it's the problem related to not really owning the product that you buy online... Books, movies, anime, songs, games, apps; anything can disappear in a blink of an eye from online libraries. We need a way to keep our purchased products after the platform/company that sold them choose to end their support. For games and apps, maybe we can have a off-line mode, or just the legal permission to modify their code in order to keep them running in our device, while most of other products such as music, books and movies can just be saved locally. We should be able to keep using what we bought, no need for more updates, just leave us with our old product. Thanks for your attention!
